title: Hyppönen — narrow vs wide AI, F-Secure's 2005 ML lab, and the 'just switch it off' fallacy
html:
Next page of If It's Smart, It's Vulnerable. Hyppönen argues today's AI is narrow — license-plate readers and Google Search are good at one thing; humans drive cars and write poetry and raise kids on the side. He notes F-Secure (his employer) has been doing "automated analysis" since 2005 — Finnish modesty kept them from calling it ML/AI like American rivals did. Closes with the "switch it off" fallacy: a supreme AI would anticipate human counter-moves.

TL;DR

  • Narrow vs wide intelligence — current AI excels at single tasks; general intelligence is what humans uniquely have.
  • Hyppönen ties the AI rebrand to marketing: F-Secure had self-learning malware-analysis pipelines in 2005 but called it "automation."
  • "Just unplug a superintelligent AI" is naïve — anticipation problem.

mikko hypponen · if its smart its vulnerable · chatgpt · malware analysis lab · deepseek
